content:
- Executed task 08 directly from NOW EXECUTE state, validated blocker tasks were done, and verified HA worker wiring/tests were already present.
- Ran focused tests and full gates; first make test failed in process real-job tests (state channel closed) and pginfo standby setup. Created two bug task files via add-bug workflow with repro traces before continuing.
- Fixed process test helper by returning/retaining ProcessState subscriber across bootstrap_and_start lifecycle, and hardened pginfo replica test by removing brittle ALTER SYSTEM batch setup plus adding bounded retry polling for replica readiness.
- Re-ran full required sequence successfully: make check, make test, make test-bdd, make lint.
- Updated task markdown status/checklists to done/passing, appended new learnings to AGENTS.md, ran task_switch script, and now need to finalize commit with evidence-rich message.

- Finalized task 08 with commit e4814a5.
- Final gate results (sequential): make check PASS, make test PASS, make test-bdd PASS, make lint PASS.
- Bookkeeping completed: task file marked done/passing with all checkboxes ticked, AGENTS.md updated with two new learnings, task_switch executed successfully.
- Created two bug task artifacts during earlier failing gate run, as required by policy.

- Investigated why panic!/expect slipped through linting.
- Root causes found:
  - src/lib.rs uses cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...)) so deny rules are disabled in test cfg.
  - src/test_harness/mod.rs has explicit allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used, clippy::panic).
  - Makefile lint target runs --all-targets with only -D warnings; clippy restriction lints (panic/expect/unwrap) are allow-by-default, so not denied there.
  - strict -D clippy::... pass is only for --lib, which excludes cfg(test) modules and integration test crates.
- Next: explain this succinctly to user with file references and optional hardening options.

- Read progress log (no prior content printed) and inspected .ralph/current_tasks.md fully.
- Found multiple <passes>false</passes> tasks and no <passes>meta-task</passes> entries.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/bugs/real-binary-tests-fail-when-port-allocation-is-blocked.md as highest-priority next task because it appears to block multiple failing suites (port allocation failures cascading into pginfo/process/etcd3 real-binary tests).
- Wrote only that path to .ralph/current_task.txt per instructions and stopped.

- User requested root-cause from .ralph/progress history before patching lint policy.
- Reviewed .ralph/progress/*.jsonl, task 05a record, and git history for src/lib.rs/src/test_harness/mod.rs/Makefile.
- Found historical rationale: task scope explicitly targeted runtime-only strict denies with narrowly scoped test exceptions; implementation commit and diary both explicitly say they avoided broad test rewrites by using cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...)) plus test_harness allow.
- Next: report concise chronology with file references and ask whether to switch policy to deny in tests too.

- User clarified they do not tolerate panic/expect in tests and asked difficulty + replacement shape.
- Prepared answer: this is medium-large but mostly mechanical; majority replacements are Result-returning tests with ?, assert/matches assertions, and helper APIs returning Result instead of panic.
- Plan if asked to execute: refactor test modules by category first, then turn on strict clippy denies for test targets to keep it enforced.

- Started by reading progress history and active task pointer from .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Active task is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/real-binary-tests-fail-when-port-allocation-is-blocked.md`.
- Per workflow, stayed in planning phase only (no implementation edits) and did broad parallel exploration across harness + pginfo + process + gate scripts.
- Ran targeted repro tests in parallel; at that moment, the previously listed failing tests passed individually.
- Ran full `make test` in isolation; it failed with two pginfo real-PG tests (`step_once_transitions_unreachable_to_primary...` and `step_once_maps_replica_when_polling_standby`) instead of the original `allocate_ports EPERM` symptom.
- Noted that this indicates behavior drift/mismatch between bug description and current observed failures, likely requiring reconciliation at execution time before coding.
- Rewrote the active bug task markdown to include a complete execution plan with phases: scope reconciliation, deep root-cause investigation, deterministic fix design, implementation hardening, validation gates, and closeout.
- Added explicit plan constraints: no optional/skipped real-binary tests, typed/clear error handling, sequential required gates.
- Added terminal marker `TO BE VERIFIED` at end of task file and stopped further work as requested.
- Next immediate action for next turn: perform deep skeptical verification, change at least one plan item, then replace marker with `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Used add-task-as-agent skill to create a new high-priority task for zero-tolerance panic/unwrap/expect policy.
- Added task file: .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/05c-task-zero-panic-unwrap-expect-across-runtime-and-tests.md
- Task includes exhaustive per-file checklist, explicit lint enforcement updates, and hard acceptance criteria requiring zero matches for panic/expect/unwrap and zero clippy allow exceptions.
- Included make gate criteria and proper Rust Result-handling expectations for all test/runtime refactors.
